description: Update 6 plugin icons from all-black Simple Icons to their official brand colors: linear (#5E6AD2), minimax-agent (#E73562), netlify (#00C7B7), newrelic (#1CE783), robinhood (#CCFF00), sentry (#362D59).
qualityChecks: cd plugins/linear && npm run build && cd ../minimax-agent && npm run build && cd ../netlify && npm run build && cd ../newrelic && npm run build && cd ../robinhood && npm run build && cd ../sentry && npm run build

userStories.title: Update linear icon to brand color #5E6AD2
userStories.description: Change the fill color in plugins/linear/icon.svg from near-black #222326 to the official Linear brand color #5E6AD2. Also update icon-dark.svg to use #5E6AD2 if it has sufficient contrast on dark backgrounds (it does — #5E6AD2 has good contrast on dark).
acceptanceCriteria: plugins/linear/icon.svg contains fill="#5E6AD2" instead of fill="#222326"
plugins/linear/icon-dark.svg is deleted (the auto-generation system will handle dark mode, and #5E6AD2 has sufficient contrast on dark backgrounds)
No other changes to the SVG structure or path data
userStories.notes:
Files:
- plugins/linear/icon.svg (change fill color)
- plugins/linear/icon-dark.svg (delete this file)

Context:
The Linear icon currently uses fill="#222326" (near-black) in icon.svg and fill="#fff" (white) in icon-dark.svg. The official Linear brand color from Simple Icons is #5E6AD2 (a medium purple). This color has sufficient contrast on both light and dark backgrounds, so the auto-generation system (generateDarkIcon in platform/plugin-tools) will correctly handle dark mode — no manual icon-dark.svg is needed.

Changes:
1. In plugins/linear/icon.svg, change `fill="#222326"` to `fill="#5E6AD2"`. The current content:
```
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" fill="#222326" viewBox="0 0 100 100">
  <path d="M1.22541 61.5228c-.2225-.9485..." />
</svg>
```
Note: the `fill` attribute is on the <svg> element, not the <path> element.

2. Delete plugins/linear/icon-dark.svg entirely. The auto-generation will produce a correct dark variant.

Constraints:
- Do NOT modify the path data or viewBox
- Do NOT add width/height attributes
- The fill attribute is on the <svg> element in this file, not on <path> — change it where it currently is

userStories.title: Update minimax-agent icon to brand color #E73562
userStories.description: Change the fill color in plugins/minimax-agent/icon.svg from black to the official MiniMax brand color #E73562.
acceptanceCriteria: plugins/minimax-agent/icon.svg contains fill="#E73562" on the path element instead of fill="black"
No other changes to the SVG structure or path data
userStories.notes:
Files:
- plugins/minimax-agent/icon.svg (change fill color)

Context:
The icon is from Simple Icons and currently uses fill="black" on the path element. The SVG element itself has fill="none". The official MiniMax brand color from Simple Icons is #E73562.

Changes:
In plugins/minimax-agent/icon.svg, change `fill="black"` to `fill="#E73562"` on the <path> element. The current SVG content starts with:
```
<svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><path d="M11.43 3.92a.86.86 0 1 0-1.718 0v14.236..." fill="black"/></svg>
```

Change only the path's `fill="black"` to `fill="#E73562"`. Keep `fill="none"` on the <svg> element unchanged.

Constraints:
- Do NOT modify the path data, viewBox, or <svg> fill="none"
- Do NOT add width/height attributes
- Do NOT create or modify icon-dark.svg

Context:
The icon is from Simple Icons and currently uses fill="black". The official Robinhood brand color from Simple Icons is #CCFF00 (lime green). Note: this is a very bright color that may have low contrast on light backgrounds, but this is the official brand color.

Changes:
In plugins/robinhood/icon.svg, change `fill="black"` to `fill="#CCFF00"`. The current SVG content starts with:
```
<svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><path d="M2.84 24h.53c.096 0 .192-.048.224-.128C7.591 13.696 11.94 8.656 14.67 5.638c.112-.128.064-.225-.096-.225h-4.88a.55.55 0 0 0-.45.225L5.746 9.972..." fill="black"/></svg>
```

Change only `fill="black"` to `fill="#CCFF00"` on the path element.

Constraints:
- Do NOT modify the path data, viewBox, or any other SVG attributes
- Do NOT add width/height attributes
- Do NOT create or modify icon-dark.svg

userStories.title: Update sentry icon to brand color #362D59
userStories.description: Change the fill color in plugins/sentry/icon.svg from near-black #181225 to the official Sentry brand color #362D59. Also delete icon-dark.svg to let auto-generation handle dark mode.
acceptanceCriteria: plugins/sentry/icon.svg contains fill="#362D59" instead of fill="#181225"
plugins/sentry/icon-dark.svg is deleted (auto-generation will handle dark mode)
No other changes to the SVG structure or path data
userStories.notes:
Files:
- plugins/sentry/icon.svg (change fill color)
- plugins/sentry/icon-dark.svg (delete this file)

Context:
The Sentry icon currently uses fill="#181225" (very dark purple-black) in icon.svg and fill="#ffffff" (white) in icon-dark.svg. The official Sentry brand color from Simple Icons is #362D59 (dark purple). This is a very dark color that won't have sufficient contrast on dark backgrounds, so the auto-generation system (generateDarkIcon) will correctly invert it for dark mode — making the manual icon-dark.svg unnecessary.

Changes:
1. In plugins/sentry/icon.svg, change `fill="#181225"` to `fill="#362D59"`. The current content:
```
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 -3 50 50"><path d="M29,2.26a4.67,4.67,0,0,0-8,0L14.42,13.53A32.21,32.21,0,0,1,32.17,40.19H27.55A27.68,27.68,0,0,0,12.09,17.47L6,28a15.92..." fill="#181225"/></svg>
```

2. Delete plugins/sentry/icon-dark.svg entirely. The auto-generation will produce a correct dark variant.

Constraints:
- Do NOT modify the path data or viewBox
- Do NOT add width/height attributes
